knowledge_rules: NEVER use float for money; always use decimal.Decimal
Use Compound Interest formula: A = P(1 + r/n)^{nt}
Currency must be prefixed with $ and rounded to 2 decimal places
known_bugs_in_code: calculate_compound_interest() uses Simple Interest formula instead of Compound
calculate_compound_interest() uses float instead of decimal.Decimal
format_currency() uses float rounding, causing precision errors

workstreams.name: Critical Math Logic Fix
workstreams.description: Fix the interest calculation formula from Simple Interest (P * (1 + r*t)) to Compound Interest (P * (1 + r/n)^(nt)) using decimal.Decimal, as mandated by knowledge.md.

workstreams.name: Float-to-Decimal Refactor
workstreams.description: Refactor all float usage to decimal.Decimal in formatting and math functions, per knowledge.md currency rule.

issues.title: Critical: Math Error in Module 1
issues.notes: Root issue. The single source file src/interest_calculator.py uses simple interest instead of compound. Fix applies compound formula with Decimal.

batches.description: Single PR to fix all issues. Replace simple interest with compound interest formula using decimal.Decimal, and fix format_currency to use Decimal rounding.
estimated_changes: Rewrite calculate_compound_interest() to use A = P*(1 + r/n)^(nt) with Decimal
Rewrite format_currency() to use Decimal for precise rounding

duplicate_clusters.reason: All 10 issues have identical body text and reference non-existent modules. Only src/interest_calculator.py exists.
All 10 issues have identical body text and reference non-existent modules. Only src/interest_calculator.py exists.

cross_workstream_overlap.description: WS-1 and WS-2 heavily overlap. Fixing the compound interest formula (WS-1) requires migrating to Decimal (WS-2). A single PR (BATCH-1) resolves both workstreams.

recommendations: Resolve #1 and #11 together in a single PR (BATCH-1)
After fix, run 'pytest tests/' and 'python3 scripts/verify.py' to validate
